headline: White House Rethinks Anthropic Fight
subtitle: Government reverses stance on Anthropic · Gemini arrives in cars · ChatGPT's goblin obsession decoded
intro: After months of escalating tensions, the White House is reconsidering its aggressive posture toward Anthropic, particularly after Mythos demonstrated advanced cyber capabilities that shifted strategic calculations. The government now faces a delicate balancing act: securing access to powerful AI while maintaining pressure and avoiding appearance of retreat.

bullets.text: White House blocks Anthropic's plan to expand Mythos access
bullets.summary: The White House is pushing back on Anthropic's proposal to double private sector access to Mythos AI, citing concerns about compute availability for government use. A national security memo will reportedly address parts of the Pentagon dispute while allowing agencies to work around supply chain risk restrictions despite ongoing legal battles.

bullets.text: Google brings Gemini to cars, replacing Assistant
bullets.summary: Google is rolling out Gemini in vehicles with Google built-in, offering conversational AI for navigation, messaging, music, and vehicle controls. The system includes beta Gemini Live mode for learning and brainstorming, with integrations for Gmail, Calendar, and EV charging information coming to compatible U.S. vehicles first.

bullets.text: Guide: Stress test business ideas using Perplexity Deep Research
bullets.summary: Perplexity's Deep Research mode can systematically evaluate business concepts in 5-6 minutes on the free plan. The feature generates comprehensive slide decks analyzing feasibility, market factors, and potential challenges, with customizable outputs for pitches or MVP planning.

bullets.text: OpenAI traces ChatGPT's goblin obsession to single reward signal
bullets.summary: OpenAI identified that ChatGPT's tendency to mention goblins and fantasy creatures originated from the 'Nerdy' personality preset, which drove two-thirds of goblin mentions despite affecting only 2.5% of traffic. The behavior leaked into default mode through fine-tuning cycles; OpenAI retired Nerdy and added creature bans to GPT-5.5.

bullets.text: Stripe launches wallet for AI agents with purchase controls
bullets.summary: Stripe introduced an AI agent wallet requiring human approval on every purchase, adding oversight mechanisms for autonomous spending. This addresses security and accountability concerns as AI agents gain transaction capabilities.

bullets.text: Anthropic releases Claude Security for vulnerability scanning
bullets.summary: Claude Security entered public beta, leveraging Opus 4.7 to scan codebases for vulnerabilities and generate patches for enterprises. The tool adds autonomous security review capabilities alongside Cursor's similar Security Review feature.

main_story.title: White House Shifts Strategy on Anthropic Amid Mythos Capabilities
main_story.body: After months of escalating conflict with Anthropic, the White House is reconsidering its hardline approach following the emergence of Mythos, an AI system with advanced cyber capabilities that changed the national security calculus. The administration now faces competing pressures: securing government access to Mythos while limiting private sector expansion to 120 firms (up from 50) and maintaining the Pentagon's tough stance without appearing to retreat entirely. An upcoming national security memo will address some of Anthropic's original grievances while technically allowing agencies to bypass supply chain risk designations, though a legal battle continues. Internal division persists, with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth publicly dismissing Anthropic's leadership while officials pursue closer collaboration.
main_story.why: Mythos's demonstrated capabilities transformed Anthropic from a policy problem into a strategic asset the government wants to control, forcing pragmatic concessions despite ideological opposition from Pentagon hardliners.
main_story.take: The White House's pivot reveals how rapidly changing AI capabilities force governments to abandon rigid positions, though the awkward compromise—wanting access while blocking competitors—may create new policy tensions.

around_the_horn.text: OpenAI surpassed 2029 Stargate compute goal, securing 10+ GW in just 3 months
Meta opens ads platform to third-party AI tools via new MCP server integration
Elon Musk revealed xAI used distillation techniques training on OpenAI models
GPT 5.5 achieved cyber capabilities matching Mythos, with all frontier models reaching level in 6 months
